notes: Neo Discovery (neo2, released June 2001) is early WOTC era (pre-2002, Base..Neo) — 11-card packs, NO reverse-holo slot. Confirmed pack structure across multiple sources: 7 common + 3 uncommon + 1 rare-or-better slot, with a holographic card inserted in ~1 of every 3 packs. The Flipside Gaming rarity review confirms the WOTC-era ~1:3 holo rate ('Holo approximately 1:3 packs', with one guaranteed Rare per pack). The single rare slot resolves to rare-holo (~1/3 of packs, the HIT bucket) or non-holo rare (~2/3). Distribution: rare-holo 0.333 + rare 0.667 = 1.0 (mapped onto the set's exactly-matching {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common} buckets). Hit bucket = rare-holo (17 holos in the 75-card set). Confidence=medium: pack structure is multiply-confirmed and the ~1:3 holo rate is a well-documented WOTC-era baseline, but no large per-set Neo Discovery pull-rate study exists. Booster box = 36 packs; NO ETB existed in this era. Sealed Neo Discovery boxes/packs carry a large vintage collector premium, so EV/booster will sit well below sealed pack price — expected for vintage, not a rate error.
